hero.title: Hydraulic Tripod
hero.subtitle: Hydraulic Damping Gimbal | Stable Support | Lightweight and Portable | More Flexible Angles

bestCompanion.subtitle: From balcony to wilderness, set up in one go, ready to shoot anytime
bestCompanion.title: DWARF's Best Shooting Partner
bestCompanion.description: Provides stable support and smooth pitch/pan adjustments, making long-duration shooting, time-lapse, and observation more reassuring.

equatorialMode.subtitle: Two modes, switch at will
equatorialMode.title: Supports equatorial mount mode
equatorialMode.description: Freely switch between equatorial mount mode and altazimuth mount mode. Equatorial mount mode is used for more stable long-term tracking, making shooting more relaxed; altazimuth mount mode is suitable for quick pointing and daily browsing, lift to see and adjust at any time.

portable.subtitle: Five-section leg tube, compact size
portable.title: Super easy to carry
portable.description: Made of aluminum alloy, it folds down to 25 cm in length. Weighing as little as 790 grams, it's easy to carry. City stargazing, travel camping, chasing the sun or the moon—no longer a 'hassle'.

stable.subtitle: Stable support for cleaner images
stable.title: Super Stable Shooting
stable.description: Solid tripod legs and stable support. Supports 360° panning and -80°/+90° tilt for easy perspective switching; securely holds the DWARF, minimizing the impact of subtle vibrations on the image.

hydraulicHead.subtitle: Smooth rotation, pushes into place with ease
hydraulicHead.title: Hydraulic head, smooth and controllable
hydraulicHead.description: Built-in hydraulic damping system ensures every adjustment is precise and smooth. No sudden jolts, no awkward pauses—confident control, precise framing.

flexibility.subtitle: Composition without constraints
flexibility.title: More flexible angles
flexibility.description:
The handle adjusts up and down, and the tripod supports multi-angle folding legs, allowing you to choose high or low positions as you wish, with no more restricted perspectives.
From looking up to capture stars to low-angle landscape shots, and even side compositions, you can always find the ideal angle.
